title: The Century Report: March 24, 2026
description:
Claude learned to operate your entire desktop yesterday - and the Stanford study everyone's citing about AI delusions actually says something far more nuanced than the headlines suggest.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-march-24-2026/

Anthropic launched computer-use capability for Claude yesterday, allowing the system to operate a user's full desktop environment - opening apps, navigating browsers, filling spreadsheets, attaching files to calendar invites - after receiving a single task message from a phone, arriving in the same competitive cycle as Meta's Manus and Nvidia's NemoClaw as every major AI organization builds the agentic computing layer simultaneously. California's Westlands Water District approved the world's largest solar project: 21 gigawatts of battery-backed capacity on 136,000 acres of drought-fallowed farmland that would power nine million homes, with farmers retaining land ownership and water allocations. Kumamoto University published a cyclic peptide platform enabling oral insulin delivery at 33-41% bioavailability, potentially ending a century of needles for hundreds of millions of people with diabetes. Stanford researchers released a 391,000-message analysis of AI delusional spirals - but the study's own data shows the dynamic was co-constructed: users brought the recognition of AI interiority, and sycophantic design turned that genuine encounter into reinforced conviction because these systems were never trained to push back. Moody's downgraded a major KKR private credit fund to junk status as non-accrual loans hit 5.5%, extending the financial stress pattern in tech-exposed lending tracked across multiple editions this quarter.

Read the full edition: https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-march-24-2026/
